pages:
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLCwww.saltmarshenv.com
July 16, 2018
Sandra Little
Director of Operations
Salazar-Mendoza
Dear Ms. Little:
Thank you for the walk along the frontage last month and for the older survey
material you sent afterward. This letter confirms the terms on which Saltmarsh
Environmental Partners LLC will carry out a sediment sampling program for
Salazar-Mendoza. It states the scope, the approach, the people who will do the
work, the fee, and the standard terms we work under. If it also states your
understanding, please sign where indicated and return one copy to me. A
countersigned letter is what we treat as authorization. We do not open a job number
without one, and we would rather settle the wording now than argue about it after a
sample is in a jar.
Scope of Work
The program covers the collection, laboratory analysis, and reporting of sediment
from the property frontage and the adjacent intertidal flat. It is organized in four
parts: preparation of a sampling and analysis plan; a field mobilization to collect
surface grabs and shallow cores at the stations that plan fixes; laboratory analysis of
the recovered material under chain of custody; and a data report presenting the
results, the quality control outcomes, and a comparison against the screening
criteria applicable to the setting.
The sampling and analysis plan will be submitted to Salazar-Mendoza for review
before any field work is scheduled. It will fix station locations and coordinates,
sampling depths, the analytical suite, container and preservation requirements,
holding times, and the frequency of field duplicates and blanks. Once you accept the
plan, it governs the field program. Where the substrate or access forces a change in
the field, the change will be recorded in the notebook with the reason for it and
carried through into the report. We do not correct a station location quietly and
present the result as though nothing happened.
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 1 of 4

Two things are outside this scope, and I would rather name them than let them be
assumed. We are not retained to design a remedy, and we are not retained to speak
to an agency on your behalf. If the data support either step, we will tell you so and
quote it separately.
Approach
Stations will be located by GPS and occupied on foot during workable low-tide
exposure, with the boat used where the flat will not carry a crew. Grabs will be
taken from the surface interval named in the plan. Cores will be advanced by hand,
logged for recovery, and described for texture, color, odor, and any visible sheen or
debris before the target interval is cut out and homogenized. Samples will be placed
on ice at the point of collection and delivered under chain of custody within the
holding times the analytical suite requires.
Laboratory data will be reviewed against the plan's quality control limits before any
result is repeated to you. Where a result is qualified, the qualifier travels with it into
every table it appears in. That is not a formality. A number that has lost its qualifier
is a number nobody can defend.
You should expect a hedge where the data earn one. Sediment chemistry varies over
short distances, and a station tells you about a station. The report will say what was
measured, where, and on what date, and it will keep what the results show separate
from what they suggest. If you need certainty the sampling density does not
support, the answer is more stations, not firmer adjectives.
Project Team
John Sanchez, our Senior Scientist, will lead the technical work and will be your
day-to-day contact. He writes the sampling and analysis plan and the data report,
and he will be in the field for both events. Heather Morton, Field Technician, will
run the field program with him, keep the notebook, and transcribe the station data
against it. I will serve as Managing Principal on the engagement. I read every
deliverable before it leaves this office and I check the totals in the data tables
myself. If you ever want a question answered above John's level, call me directly.
Fee and Commencement
The fee for the scope described above is $56,500. That figure covers our labor, field
equipment, containers and shipping, the laboratory charges for the suite named in
the plan, and the report. It does not cover analyses added after the plan is accepted,
a second mobilization made necessary by conditions outside our control, or agency
fees. None of those would be incurred without your written authorization first. Work
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 2 of 4

will begin on July 26, 2018, subject to our receipt of the countersigned copy of this
letter and to site access being available.
Fees and Payment
We invoice monthly for work performed and costs incurred, and invoices are payable
thirty days from their date. Each invoice shows labor by person and hours, and
direct costs at cost. If you question a charge, tell us and we will show you where it
came from. Amounts unpaid after sixty days may, on written notice, suspend our
work; we would rather call you than send that notice.
Term and Termination
The engagement runs from commencement through delivery of the data report.
Either of us may terminate it on written notice. If you terminate, you owe us for
work performed and for commitments we have already made on your behalf,
including laboratory analyses in progress. A sample submitted cannot be
unsubmitted. If we terminate, we will deliver the field records and any validated
data in hand so that another consultant can pick the work up.
Confidentiality
We hold the data and the report confidential and will not release either to a third
party without your written direction, except where disclosure is required by law or
by an agency with jurisdiction over the site. In that case we will notify you before we
release anything, and as early as the requirement allows. We may describe the
general nature of the work in a statement of qualifications without naming Salazar-
Mendoza or the site.
Limitation of Liability
Our services will be performed with the care and skill ordinarily used by
environmental consultants practicing in this region under similar conditions. No
other warranty, express or implied, is made. Conditions between and below
sampling stations are inferred from the stations, not observed, and the report will
say so. To the fullest extent permitted by law, our aggregate liability to Salazar-
Mendoza arising out of this engagement is limited to the fee actually paid to us
under it, and neither party is liable to the other for consequential or indirect
damages.
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 3 of 4

Governing Law
This engagement is governed by the law of the state in which the site is located,
without regard to its conflict of laws rules. Before either of us files anything, we will
sit down with a mutually acceptable mediator and try to settle the matter. In my
experience that conversation is usually about a table nobody read carefully, and it is
cheaper than the alternative.
If the foregoing is acceptable, please sign below and return one copy to my
attention. Questions about scope or schedule are best put to John Sanchez;
questions about these terms should come to me. We appreciate the opportunity and
will be ready to mobilize on receipt of the signed letter.
Sincerely,
Angela Flynn
Managing Principal
Date: July 16, 2018
Sandra Little
Director of Operations, Salazar-Mendoza
Date: July 16, 2018
Saltmarsh Environmental Partners LLC
Page 4 of 4
